filters.notes: Evaluated FIRST in inner loop. int8_t read (1 byte). Rejects 79% of rows immediately — most rejecting filter. Use unsigned trick: (uint8_t)(disc - 5) <= 2u to fuse two comparisons into one.
Evaluated SECOND. int8_t read (1 byte). Only 21% of rows reach this check. Rejects a further ~53%.

Evaluated THIRD. int32_t read (4 bytes). Only ~10% of rows reach this check. Within interior qualifying blocks, ~99% pass — use __builtin_expect(true) hint. Use unsigned range trick: (uint32_t)(sd - 8766) < (uint32_t)(9131 - 8766) to fuse two comparisons.

scan.madvise_strategy: After zone map prune identifies [row_lo, row_hi), call madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) on just the qualifying byte ranges of each column file before launching threads. Do NOT use MADV_SEQUENTIAL on the full file — that wastes kernel readahead on 800MB of unneeded data.

layout.entries: ZoneEntry[num_blocks] where ZoneEntry = { int32_t min_date; int32_t max_date; } (8B each)

indexes.usage: Load entire zone map at startup (fits in L1 cache). Linear scan of 600 entries to find contiguous window [block_lo, block_hi). Skip block if zone.max_date < 8766 OR zone.min_date >= 9131.

data_structures.thread_accumulator: double (per-thread local, cache-line padded struct to avoid false sharing)
data_structures.final_reduction: sequential sum of nthreads thread-local doubles

aggregation.notes: Each thread accumulates into a local double. No hash table. Final result = sum of all thread-local accumulators.

parallelism.partition_unit: row_range within qualifying block window [row_lo, row_hi)
parallelism.notes: Divide [row_lo, row_hi) evenly across nthreads. Each thread does its own per-block zone check within its assigned range, then applies the triple filter in order (disc → qty → sd) and accumulates revenue. Use cache-line-padded partial[] array (struct { double val; char pad[56]; }) to avoid false sharing.

inner_loop.description: Per-row logic within qualifying blocks — CRITICAL: evaluate in this order
filter_tricks: Discount: (uint8_t)(disc - 5) <= 2u — fuses >= 5 AND <= 7 into single unsigned comparison
Shipdate: (uint32_t)(sd - 8766) < 365u — fuses >= 8766 AND < 9131 into one unsigned subtraction+compare
Use __builtin_expect on shipdate check: __builtin_expect((uint32_t)(sd-8766) >= 365u, 0) since ~99% pass
inner_loop.notes: l_discount and l_quantity are int8_t (1 byte each, cheap read). l_shipdate is int32_t (4 bytes, read only after 21%×47%≈10% pass the int8 gates). l_extendedprice (8 bytes double) read only for the ~10% that pass all three. This order reduces average bytes read from 6.0 to 2.4 per row — a 60% reduction in memory traffic per qualifying-block row.

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category A — plan restructuring): the iter_0 inner loop evaluates filters in the order l_shipdate → l_discount → l_quantity, but within qualifying blocks l_shipdate passes ~99% of the time (interior blocks are sorted entirely within [8766,9131)), making it the least selective gate despite being checked first. Reordering to l_discount (21% pass) → l_quantity (47%) → l_shipdate (99%) means 79% of rows are eliminated after reading only 1 byte (discount), reducing average memory traffic per row from 6.0 bytes to 2.4 bytes (60% reduction) and average branch evaluations per row from 2.2 to 1.3. Both effects reduce CPU cycles for the hot cache-resident scan. Additional improvements: (1) use unsigned-arithmetic range tricks to fuse each two-sided comparison into one branch; (2) use MADV_WILLNEED on the qualifying row range of each column instead of MADV_SEQUENTIAL on the full files, avoiding 800MB of useless kernel readahead on cold runs; (3) cache-line-pad the per-thread partial[] accumulator struct to eliminate false sharing.
